title: trachyspermum carvifolium
intro: Trachyspermum carvifolium, also known as ajwain, is a small annual herb native to the Middle East and the Indian subcontinent. The medicinal properties of this plant include its carminative and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues such as bloating, gas, and indigestion. The health benefits of ajwain include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use in treating menstrual cramps and other women's health issues. Ajwain is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed on their own or used as an ingredient in various recipes.
